She has a history of multiple surgeries and health issues, but nothing points to a surgical cause for her symptoms.
Considering all this, the most likely diagnosis is late-onset adrenal hyperplasia, specifically the virilizing form.
This condition causes the adrenal glands to produce excess androgens, leading to the symptoms she's experiencing.
The normal DHEAS and CT scan help narrow it down to this condition rather than other causes like adrenal tumors or enzyme defects.
